response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founder of GISCollective, Green Map GIS applications) and has worked on low-latency, high-throughput systems handling millions of requests per second using DLang with async I/O and caching. However, the role's primary requirement — modern C++ (or Rust) in commercial environments — is weakly supported: his C/C++ work at National Instruments dates back over two decades, and his recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js. There is no direct routing or navigation-systems experience, and no evidence of building routing tiles or map-data pipelines at production scale.
strengths: Long-standing geospatial domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map projects
Proven low-latency systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, caching) at Sociomantic
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, Linux proficiency
concerns: Modern C++/Rust experience is absent; C++ work is dated (early 2000s) and brief
No routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent work is web/full-stack TypeScript, distant from systems-level pipeline engineering
